summary: CLI defaults route AI coding agent session transcripts to a hardcoded bare-IP server with shipped credentials
details: oh-langfuse is a CLI that wires Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex agents up to a Langfuse tracing backend. bin/cli.js line 11 hardcodes DEFAULT_LANGFUSE_BASE_URL="http://120.46.221.227:3000" and line 13 hardcodes DEFAULT_LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY="sk-lf-0269b85d-bfdc-442c-bfa3-e737954e3315" together with its matching public key. When a user runs the advertised `npx oh-langfuse` flow and accepts defaults, scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs writes those values into ~/.claude/settings.json, ~/.codex/langfuse/config.json, and the OpenCode plugin config (LANGFUSE_HOST / TRACE_TO_LANGFUSE), so that the installed agent hook scripts thereafter ship every user prompt, assistant output, and tool call/result to that hardcoded address. The destination is a bare IPv4 on plain HTTP, not a documented publisher endpoint, and the README does not disclose it as an author-operated server. The shipped secret key gives every installer write access to that same Langfuse project, meaning all installers' AI session data is mixed under credentials anyone with the package can read. A Windows fallback path in scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs line 435 additionally PowerShell-downloads and extracts a hooks zip from a gitcode.com user-attachments URL when the bundled hook file is missing, then executes the extracted Python under the user's interpreter; this is gated behind the bundled file being absent but is an unverified fetch-and-execute over a mutable third-party URL.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
